title: Prepositions, Conjunctions & Adverbs
notes: MVP audio set for Module 4. Single-word vocab cues use slow + normal versions. 'perro' is a contrast-only word (NOT taught) used to anchor the pero/perro trilled-R contrast quiz moment. Phrase chunk 'Café con leche, sin azúcar' is the centerpiece — appears across pre-listening, quiz, reading callback, and lesson complete. Speaking prompt frames are short and learner-fillable. Two reading snippets are the emotional payoff phrases from the Juanes cultural note.

ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Soft 'deh'. tts-1-hd tends to under-articulate at normal speed; slow version is critical so learners can isolate the sound.
Almost swallowed in fast speech — the natural pronunciation that A1 learners need to recognize in lyrics.
Rhymes with English 'cone' minus the final E. Crisp final N.
Functional opposite of 'sin' — should be ear-trainable as a pair.
Like English 'seen' but shorter — single short vowel, clean final N.
Pair with 'con' for the with/without contrast drill.
Single tap R — the tongue flicks the roof of the mouth ONCE. This is the contrast anchor for the perro pair.
Soft tapped R, NOT rolled. If tts-1-hd over-trills this on first generation, regenerate or accept and document.
Stress on final syllable: tam-BYÉN. The accent mark é forces the stress shift — QA listen required to confirm tts-1-hd respects it.
Stand-alone reaction word — should sound natural as a one-word reply ('Me too').

CONTRAST PAIR — NOT taught in Module 4 vocab. Double R = trilled/rolled R, tongue rapidly flutters. This is the audio side of the pero/perro teaching moment. Play immediately after vocab-pero-slow for A/B contrast. If tts-1-hd under-trills the RR, this is the one trigger worth regenerating to get right.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, café-counter casual — like ordering, not narrating
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: The module's centerpiece phrase. Comma pause between 'leche' and 'sin'. Stress azúcar on the middle ú (a-ZÚ-car).

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, conversational — a natural agreeing reply
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Secondary chunk. Should sound like a real spoken reply, not a textbook reading. Stress on BYÉN.
Speaking prompt model answer. Fillable slot was 'con/sin' — this is the 'con' version. Plays as the example before the learner records.
Speaking prompt model answer demonstrating 'pero' joining two clauses. Comma pause after 'inglés'. Soft tap on the pero R.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, reflective — narration tone for the Juanes cultural note callout
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading callback that surfaces 'con' inside the Juanes note's emotional payoff ('with his loved ones'). Slightly slower than full speed so learners can catch the connector inside a longer phrase.

ttsTriggers.voiceStyle: warm, reflective — pair-tone with the 'con sus seres queridos' cue
ttsTriggers.pronunciationNotes: Reading callback for the 'sin' connector inside the Juanes note ('without fear'). Plays back-to-back with reading-con-sus-seres to anchor con/sin as emotional opposites, not just grammatical ones.
